title: AGENTS.md
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate summaries, data strips, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . model implementing er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface. companion package erglm (formerly erlr) fits GLM-based exposure-response models (binomial, poisson, gaussian, Gamma) implements package’s generics model objects. erplots hard dependency erglm – ’s listed Suggests, examples/tests/vignettes. Since erglm GitHub-(CRAN), DESCRIPTION also declares Remotes: djnavarro/erglm CI can resolve . Key API points remember: example dataset erglm::erglm_data (erlr::lr_data), models fit erglm::erglm_model(formula, data, family = ...) (family now explicit – use family = binomial() binary ae1/ae2 responses example data), simulated erglm::erglm_vpc_sim().

title: AGENTS.md
text: See PLAN.md scoped-future development. main item extending several data-summary layers (quantile bins, data strips, VPC plots), currently assume binary (0/1) response, also support continuous-response exposure-response models.

title: AGENTS.md
text: R/er-generics.R – model interface: er_predict(), er_simulate(), er_summary() generics default methods. R/er-plot-api.R – public plot-building API: er_plot(), er_plot_show_model(), er_plot_show_quantiles(), er_plot_show_datastrip(), er_plot_show_groups(), er_plot_build(), plus print/plot methods er_plot S3 class. R/er-plot-part.R – internal .part_*() functions assemble configuration plot component (er_predict() / er_simulate() / er_summary() get called user-supplied model). R/er-plot-build.R, R/er-plot-compose.R – internal plotting/layout machinery turns parts ggplot2 objects composes patchwork. R/er-plot-partials-*.R – pluggable build_*() partial builders (one file per component: model, summary, quantile, datastrip, group). See ?er_partial interface builders share. R/er-vpc.R – er_vpc_plot(), model-agnostic VPC-style plot operating plain observed/simulated data frames. R/utils-helpers.R, R/utils-global.R – small internal helpers (including binary-response-clopper_pearson(), cut_quantile(), cut_exposure_quantile()) globalVariables() declarations NSE.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Document roxygen2 (devtools::document()); Markdown roxygen enabled (Roxygen: list(markdown = TRUE)). Run tests devtools::test(); full checks devtools::check(). package check cleanly (0 errors/warnings/notes). Tests live tests/testthat/. test files need erlr (Suggests dependency, used fit example models) guarded skip_if_not_installed("erlr"); shared test fixtures live tests/testthat/helper-data.R. Vignettes/articles live vignettes/articles/ built pkgdown site, shipped package (see .Rbuildignore).

title: AGENTS.md
text: Use base R pipe (|>), magrittr pipe. Follow existing tidyverse-style conventions (dplyr/tibble/rlang/ ggplot2/patchwork) already used throughout. Public API functions prefixed er_; partial builders prefixed build_; internal helpers prefixed .. Never call model-fitting function package. plot component needs something model, add extend generic R/er-generics.R instead reaching model internals.

title: erplots development plan
text: document tracks scoped-future development erplots. changelog. Items proposals reviewed implementation, committed designs.

previous_headings: Extend beyond binary responses to continuous (and count) responses
title: erplots development plan
text: erplots designed model-agnostic fitted model side (model implementing er_predict()/er_simulate()/er_summary() can plotted), several data layers still hardcode assumption observed response binary (0/1). Generalising layers let erplots visualise, e.g., continuous-response exposure-response models (dose-response/emax-style models) well logistic-regression style binary-response models, without changing model-side contract.

previous_headings: Extend beyond binary responses to continuous (and count) responses
title: erplots development plan
text: model curve/ribbon spaghetti layers (er_plot_show_model(), build_model_ribbonline(), build_model_spaghetti()) ever consume er_predict()/er_simulate() output (fit_resp/ci_lower/ci_upper, simulated draws) – ’s assumption response type anywhere code path. changes needed. group panel (er_plot_show_groups(), boxplot/violin exposure group) ever looks exposure variable, response. changes needed.

previous_headings: Extend beyond binary responses to continuous (and count) responses
title: erplots development plan
text: Quantile summary layer (er_plot_show_quantiles(), .part_quantile(), build_quantile_errorbar()): computes, per exposure bin, n1/n0 counts Clopper-Pearson CI response rate. continuous response instead compute mean CI mean (e.g. t-based, SE-based). Needs response-type-dispatched summary step – likely small internal generic (analogous existing build_* partial pattern) summarise_quantile.binary() / summarise_quantile.continuous() implementations, selected either explicit argument auto-detecting whether response column binary (values {0, 1}, logical). Data strip layer (er_plot_show_datastrip(), build_datastrip_jitter()): “responders line, non-responders ” two-panel jitter strip inherently binary-response visualisation. doesn’t obvious continuous analogue (plain exposure-vs-response scatter closest equivalent, ’s different plot, variant one). layer likely need genuinely different design continuous responses, simply offered continuous-response plots. er_vpc_plot(): currently compares observed vs. simulated response rates (Clopper-Pearson CIs observed side, simulation quantiles simulated side). Generalises fairly directly comparing means (appropriate CI observed mean) – easiest three generalise. Response-type detection/declaration: needs decision whether response type auto-detected (e.g., “binary” response column takes values {0, 1}/logical, “continuous” otherwise) explicitly declared (e.g. response_type argument er_plot()). Auto-detection convenient failure modes (e.g. 0/1 count variable isn’t actually binary response); explicit argument sensible auto-detected default probably safer choice. Count (Poisson-like) responses: clearly binary continuous. Treating “continuous” (mean + CI) probably adequate approximation first pass, worth flagging known simplification rather deciding definitively now.

previous_headings: Extend beyond binary responses to continuous (and count) responses
title: erplots development plan
text: questions originally left open; now working recommendation implementation isn’t blocked, still debate new information changes calculus. Response-type detection. Add explicit response_type argument (probably er_plot(), since governs builders valid whole plot, just one component), defaulting "auto". auto-detection heuristic: values entirely {0, 1} logical column → "binary", otherwise → "continuous". mirrors “automatic escape hatch” pattern already adopted erlr side (e.g. SCM test-selection decision) – convenient default, overridable heuristic gets wrong (e.g. genuine 0/1 count variable). Data strip continuous responses. Omit v1, documented limitation, rather design continuous-specific replacement now. two-panel “responders line, non-responders ” design structurally binary flag – ’s variant geometry continuous response, different plot entirely (e.g. rug raw scatter), building speculatively anyone needs isn’t worth design effort yet. Revisit /concrete use case shows . Quantile-bin CI method continuous responses. Use t-interval, bootstrap. mirrors convention erlr/erglm’s generalisation plan settled analogous problem (families estimated dispersion – gaussian, Gamma – use Pr(>|t|)-based inference, chi-squared/asymptotic-normal approximation), two packages stay consistent, ’s simpler implement explain bootstrap. Count responses. Treat “continuous” v1 (mean ± CI via t-interval (3)), documented known approximation. Fast follow, ’s concrete need: add exact Poisson CI path (analogous binary path uses exact Clopper-Pearson interval rather normal approximation) – machinery swapping response-type-specific interval method already exists (1)-(3) place, small addition, redesign. worth building speculatively now given erglm’s v1 family scope already treats poisson support real secondary priority. Coordination erlr/erglm. Don’t block erglm’s rename timeline. erplots’ continuous-response work needs model object implementing er_predict() continuous response test – ’s easy stub today plain glm(family = gaussian()) (even lm()) lines local er_predict.<class>()/er_summary.<class>() methods package’s test suite, without waiting erglm exist. Revisit using erglm’s actual continuous-response support lands, start work independently.

previous_headings: Extend beyond binary responses to continuous (and count) responses
title: erplots development plan
text: Decide response-type detection/declaration mechanism. Generalise quantile summary layer (binary vs. continuous paths). Generalise er_vpc_plot()’s summary statistic/CI computation. Design continuous-response equivalent (replacement) data strip layer. Expand tests/vignettes cover continuous-response models (using erglm available, plain lm()/nls() model interim hand-written er_predict() methods).

title: Plotting
text: Unlike plotting function fits model behind scenes, erplots expects fit model pass explicitly:
code: mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())

title: Plotting
text: Basic usage  Adding extra components
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(exposure = aucss, response = ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_datastrip() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = aucss) |>    plot()

title: Plotting
text: Stratification adds colour across components. requires model includes stratification variable term:  can suppress stratification specific components
code: mod_strat <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_datastrip() |>   plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(     exposure = aucss,      response = ae1,      stratify_by = sex   ) |>    # keep_strata = FALSE needs a model that doesn't include the   # stratification variable, so we pass the un-stratified `mod` here   er_plot_show_model(mod, keep_strata = FALSE) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_datastrip() |>   plot()

title: Plotting
text: Default style = "ribbonline" can also draw spaghetti plots represent parameter uncertainty. Spaghetti plots require model implement er_simulate() (erglm’s models ); models implement er_predict() fall back "ribbonline" message.
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod, style = "spaghetti") |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    plot() #> Using seed = 4371 #> Warning in ggplot2::geom_path(data = sim, mapping = ggplot2::aes(x = #> .data[[exposure$name]], : Ignoring unknown parameters: `fill`

title: Plotting
text: can modify number bins:  can also modify confidence level Clopper-Pearson interval (empirical-summary layer currently assumes binary response):
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 6) |>    plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 6, conf_level = .8) |>    plot()

title: Plotting
text: Multiple grouping variables allowed:  Stratification propagates group component:  default style = "boxplot" can also use violin plots:
code: erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, sex)) |>    plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1, stratify_by = sex) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod_strat) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = aucss) |>    plot() erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = sex, style = "violin") |>    plot()

code: @Manual{,   title = {erplots: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots},   author = {Danielle Navarro},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/djnavarro/erplots}, }

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
text: erplots provides fluent mini-language building exposure-response plots: model curves/ribbons, quantile-binned response-rate summaries, data strips, grouped distribution panels. model-agnostic: erplots never fits model . Instead, fit model whatever package suits workflow (e.g. erglm logistic regression), pass fitted object er_plot_show_model(). model implements er_predict() can visualised; implementing er_simulate() er_summary() additionally enables uncertainty spaghetti plots/VPCs summary annotations (e.g. p-values). See ?er_model_interface.

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
text: can install development version erplots like :
code: pak::pak("djnavarro/erplots")

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
code: library(erplots) library(erglm)  mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>    er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>    er_plot_show_model(mod) |>    er_plot_show_quantiles() |>    er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>    plot() mod2 <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) mod2_marginal <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  plt <- erglm_data |>     er_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>     # keep_strata = FALSE needs a model without the stratification    # variable as a term, so we pass `mod2_marginal` here    er_plot_show_model(mod2_marginal, keep_strata = FALSE) |>     er_plot_show_quantiles(bins = 3) |>     er_plot_show_datastrip() |>     er_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, treatment), keep_strata = FALSE)  print(plt) #> <er_plot> #>   plot variables: #>     - exposure:        aucss #>     - response:        ae2 #>     - stratification:  sex #>   plot components: #>     - model:           erglm_model/glm/lm #>     - quantile:        3 bins #>     - strip:           jitter both #>     - group:           .aucss_quantile, treatment #>   plots built: <none> #>   output built: no plot(plt)

title: Model-Agnostic Exposure-Response Plots
code: sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod2, seed = 1234) sim #> # A tibble: 30,000 × 5 #>      ae2 aucss sex    row_id sim_id #>    <int> <dbl> <fct>   <int>  <int> #>  1     1  673. Male        1      1 #>  2     1 2806. Female      2      1 #>  3     0    0  Female      3      1 #>  4     1 1169. Female      4      1 #>  5     0  377. Male        5      1 #>  6     1  327. Female      6      1 #>  7     0    0  Male        7      1 #>  8     1 1208. Female      8      1 #>  9     0    0  Male        9      1 #> 10     0  254. Female     10      1 #> # ℹ 29,990 more rows  er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = aucss) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = sex)

what: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: erplots draws exposure-response plots fitted model implements small interface, rather assuming particular model class (e.g. logistic regression glm). make model usable er_plot_show_model() friends, implement least method er_predict(). Implementing er_simulate() additionally enables simulation-based visualisations (e.g. spaghetti plots, VPCs); implementing er_summary() enables annotations p-value labels.

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
code: er_predict(model, newdata, conf_level = 0.95, ...)  er_simulate(model, newdata, nsim = 100, seed = NULL, ...)  er_summary(model, ...)

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: model fitted exposure-response model object newdata data frame covariate values predict conf_level Confidence level prediction interval ... Passed methods nsim Number simulation replicates seed Optional RNG seed

title: Model interface for exposure-response plots — er_model_interface
text: er_predict() returns newdata three additional columns: fit_resp (point prediction), ci_lower, ci_upper. er_simulate() returns data frame containing nsim replicates newdata, sim_id column identifying replicate, fit_resp column giving simulated prediction replicate (reflecting parameter uncertainty). Models support simulation-based visualisation implement method; default method returns NULL; callers treat NULL result "available" rather error. er_summary() returns named list scalar summary statistics (example list(p_value = 0.013)), NULL nothing available. default method returns NULL.

"model", "summary", "quantile", pieces added plot already coord sets axis limits. "datastrip" "group" plots, plot object yet coord. expectation, however, builder supply x-axis limit consistent base plot. , since component plots use exposure variable x-axis, use values stored exposure$limits tp set x-axis limits.

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a fitted model — er_plot
code: er_plot(data, exposure, response, stratify_by = NULL)  er_plot_style(object, labels)  er_plot_show_model(   object,   model,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "ribbonline",   conf_level = 0.95 )  er_plot_show_quantiles(   object,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "errorbar",   bins = 4,   conf_level = 0.95 )  er_plot_show_datastrip(   object,   keep_strata = NULL,   style = "jitter",   panel = "both" )  er_plot_show_groups(   object,   group_by,   style = "boxplot",   bins = NULL,   keep_strata = NULL )  er_plot_build(object)

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a fitted model — er_plot
text: data Observed data exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted) stratify_by Stratification variable used color fill (one variable, unquoted) object Partially constructed plot (S3 class er_plot) labels Named list labels model fitted exposure-response model. Must implement er_predict(); implementing er_simulate() er_summary() enables additional visualisations (see er_model_interface) keep_strata Logical, indicating whether component keep color stratification style Character string used specify partial builder component conf_level Confidence level bins Number exposure bins (counting placebo) panel Character string: "upper", "lower", "" (default) group_by Grouping variables define groups distribution plots (tidyselection variables)

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a fitted model — er_plot
text: Plot object class er_plot

title: Builds an exposure-response plot for a fitted model — er_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae1 ~ aucss, erglm_data, family = binomial())  erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae1) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_groups(aucss) |>   plot()  mod2 <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) plt <- erglm_data |>   er_plot(aucss, ae2, stratify_by = sex) |>   er_plot_show_model(mod2, keep_strata = FALSE) |>   er_plot_show_quantiles() |>   er_plot_show_datastrip() |>   er_plot_show_groups(group_by = c(aucss, treatment), keep_strata = FALSE)  print(plt) plot(plt) } # }

what: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: Compares observed response rates simulated response rates model, stratified grouping variable. function model-agnostic: operates purely data frames. sim data frame expected contain one row per simulated observation per replicate, sim_id column identifying replicate (see e.g. erglm::erglm_vpc_sim() one way generate simulations fitted model).

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: er_vpc_plot(data, sim, exposure, response, group_by, conf_level = 0.95)

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: data Observed data sim Simulated data, exposure/response/group_by columns data, plus sim_id column identifying replicate exposure Exposure variable (one variable, unquoted) response Response variable (one variable, unquoted). Assumed binary (0/1) group_by Variable (unquoted) stratify predictions conf_level Confidence level

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
text: ggplot2 object

title: Visual predictive check plot for an exposure-response model — er_vpc_plot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(erglm) mod <- erglm_model(ae2 ~ aucss + sex, erglm_data, family = binomial()) sim <- erglm_vpc_sim(mod) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = aucss) er_vpc_plot(erglm_data, sim, aucss, ae2, group_by = sex) } # }
